title: The Water's Own Accounting

features.name: the counting pools
features.desc-on-touch: each pool holds a different enumeration: heartbeats, rainfall, molecules, paradoxes; they do not add to the same total

features.name: the confluence counter
features.desc-on-touch: where all the incompatible tallies pour into one ledger and the water keeps its own true count, which is none of them

features.name: current-measure
features.desc-on-touch: the flow itself is the tally; each moment it recounts what has passed, and the count is always different

features.name: witness stones
features.desc-on-touch: they mark the line where one counting-system gives up and another begins; the boundary itself keeps a silent count

features.name: the drowned tally
features.desc-on-touch: numbers written on paper that sank; the water reads them as change and as duration and as the space between moments

inscription: here the water counts in a language made only of flow, and every sum is both true and false at once

file.name: Confluence_Ledger_Sections_III-VII.txt
file.note: fragments of counting systems that entered the water at different points; they reconcile themselves as they merge downstream, creating new arithmetic

exits.label: back west to where the counts first began their divergence
east where the larger water flows to the sea and carries all counts with it
upstream to where the first tallies were written
